description_long: This is a small, vintage-style biplane with a striking blue and white color scheme, featuring bold geometric patterns across its wings and fuselage. The aircraft has two sets of straight, parallel wings stacked vertically, connected by struts and wires, characteristic of classic biplane designs. The nose houses a single propeller engine, and the landing gear consists of two main wheels under the wings and a small tailwheel at the rear. The fuselage is rounded and tapers toward the tail, which features a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The cockpit is positioned just behind the upper wing, appearing to be open and likely designed for a single pilot. The overall design suggests a lightweight, maneuverable airplane suitable for aerobatics or recreational flying.
description: A small, blue and white vintage biplane with stacked wings, a single propeller, and an open cockpit. Its bold geometric patterns and classic design evoke early 20th-century aviation.
description_view_0: Front view showing the dual stacked wings, central propeller, and landing gear, with the blue and white pattern radiating from the nose.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the star insignia on the fuselage, the open cockpit, and the tailwheel, with the propeller and engine cowling visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail assembly, including the vertical and horizontal stabilizers, and the symmetry of the wing structure.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the same wing and tail configuration, with the propeller and cockpit profile visible.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two sets of straight, parallel wings stacked vertically; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two fixed main wheels and a single tailwheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open cockpit located just behind the upper wing, likely single-seat.
